summary: American Dadaist and Surrealist photographer born Emmanuel Radnitzky. Known for Le Violon d'Ingres (Kiki de Montparnasse's back with f-holes), photograms called 'rayographs,' solarization technique, and photographing Marcel Duchamp as cross-dressing alter-ego Rrose Selavy. Also made readymades including The Gift (iron with tacks) and Object to Be Destroyed (metronome with an eye). Spent most of his career in Paris.

works.name: Le Violon d'Ingres (1924)
works.description: Man Ray's most famous photograph. Depicts his model and lover Kiki de Montparnasse (Alice Prin) nude from behind, with two f-holes painted on her back to evoke a violin. Sold in May 2022 for $12.4 million, then the most expensive photograph ever sold at auction. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Kiki de Montparnasse nude from the back with two f-holes painted on her skin to evoke a violin
examples: In a photo by this man, Kiki de Montparnasse's nude back has two f-holes painted on it.
This photographer's most famous image depicts Kiki de Montparnasse nude with f-holes on her back, titled Le Violon d'Ingres.

clues.clue: Sold in May 2022 for the then-record price for a photograph at auction
examples: In May 2022, this artist's photograph Le Violon d'Ingres, which depicts Kiki de Montparnasse, became the most expensive photograph ever sold.

clues.clue: Kiki de Montparnasse (Alice Ernestine Prin), 'Queen of Montparnasse', was the model
examples: This female model posed for Man Ray's Le Violon d'Ingres. You can give either the real name of the mononym of this 'Queen of Montparnasse.'

works.name: Rayographs (Photograms)
works.description: Man Ray's camera-less photographs, made by placing objects directly on photosensitive paper and exposing them to light. He named the technique after himself: 'rayographs.' Subjects included hands, springs, rolls of film, and a slinky. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Named camera-less photographs (photograms) after himself: 'rayographs'
examples: name this American Dadaist who created namesake 'graphs' with photosensitive paper
This pioneer of the photogram painted two f-holes onto a model's back for a 1924 photograph.
This man named a class of photograms after himself, and made many of hands, springs, and rolls of film.

clues.clue: Used a slinky in one of his best-known rayographs
examples: This artist of Glass Tears used a slinky in his best-known photogram

clues.clue: Made film Return to Reason without a camera, throwing salt and pepper onto film
examples: This photographer threw salt and pepper onto a piece of film for one scene of a film he made without a camera, Return to Reason.

works.name: Noir et Blanche (1926)
works.description: Photograph of Kiki de Montparnasse resting her white head horizontally next to an upright African mask. Frequent quizbowl clue pairing the two contrasting forms. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Woman rests her white head horizontally next to an upright African mask
examples: A woman rests her head next to an upright African mask in this photographer's Noir et blanche
A frequent model of this photographer lays her head down horizontally next to a vertical African mask in Noir et Blanche.

works.name: Marcel Duchamp / Rrose Selavy Photographs
works.description: Man Ray photographed Marcel Duchamp dressed in drag as his female alter-ego Rrose Selavy. One portrait of Rrose Selavy appeared on a Belle Haleine perfume bottle label. Wikipedia

images.caption: Marcel Duchamp as Rrose Selavy (Man Ray)

clues.clue: Photographed Marcel Duchamp cross-dressed as female alter-ego Rrose Selavy
examples: Known for photographing Marcel Duchamp as Rrose Selavy
This artist photographed a cross-dressing Marcel Duchamp, his fellow Dadaist.
he also photographed Marcel Duchamp in drag as his alter-ego Rrose Selavy

clues.clue: Portrait of Rrose Selavy appeared as the label on the Belle Haleine perfume bottle
examples: his portrait of an artist wearing a hat and pearls is the label of the Belle Haleine perfume bottle

works.name: Meret Oppenheim Photographs
works.description: Man Ray photographed Meret Oppenheim nude at an etching/printing press, with ink over her palm and forearm. One photograph shows a nude woman's torso with arms raised to look like a minotaur. Wikipedia

images.caption: Meret Oppenheim at the printing press (Man Ray)

clues.clue: Photographed Meret Oppenheim nude at an etching/printing press, with ink over her palm and forearm
examples: This photographer captured another artist nude with ink over her palm and forearm, standing behind a printing wheel.
Meret Oppenheim was photographed nude at an etching press by this photographer.

works.name: Solarization Technique
works.description: Man Ray developed the solarization (Sabattier effect) technique with his lover Lee Miller — exposing a developing image to a flash of light to partially reverse tones. Lee Miller's torso appears in one of his photograms. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Developed solarization technique with lover Lee Miller — exposing a developing image to a flash of light
examples: With their lover, this photographer developed a technique of exposing a developing image to a flash of light called solarisation.
Meret Oppenheim posed nude for him in a few photographs, and he invented the solarization technique.

clues.clue: Lee Miller's torso appears in one of his solarized photograms
examples: The torso of that lover, Lee Miller, appears in one of this photographer's namesake photograms.

works.name: Object to Be Destroyed (1923/1966)
works.description: Readymade/assemblage: a metronome with a photograph of an eye attached to the pendulum. Student protesters actually destroyed the original in 1957; Man Ray then created an edition called 'Indestructible Object.' Inspired by a line from Lautréamont about the chance meeting of an umbrella and a sewing machine on a dissecting table. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Metronome with a photograph of an eye on the pendulum; actually destroyed by student protesters in 1957
examples: This Dadaist combined a metronome and a photograph of an eye into the readymade Object To Be Destroyed, which student protesters actually destroyed in 1957.

clues.clue: A sculpture by this man was inspired by Lautréamont's line about the chance meeting of an umbrella on a dissecting table
examples: A sculpture by this man was inspired by a certain novel's line about the chance meeting of an umbrella on a dissecting table.

works.name: The Enigma of Isidore Ducasse (1920)
works.description: Wrapped a sewing machine in an army blanket (or wool) and tied it with yellow string — the title refers to Isidore Ducasse (Comte de Lautréamont), from whose work the 'umbrella and dissecting table' line comes. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Wrapped a sewing machine in an army blanket/wool and tied it with yellow string
examples: He wrapped a sewing machine in an army blanket and tied it with yellow string to create The Enigma of Isidore Ducasse.
He wrapped a sewing machine in wool and string in one work.

works.name: The Gift (1921)
works.description: Readymade: an iron with a row of metal tacks glued to the bottom. Presented at the Cabaret Voltaire in Zurich. Archetype of Dadaist humor — a domestic object rendered useless. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Iron with a row of tacks attached to the bottom; presented at the Cabaret Voltaire
examples: An iron with a row of tacks attached to the bottom called The Gift was a readymade at the Cabaret by this Dada photographer

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Born Emmanuel Radnitzky in Philadelphia; spent most of his career in Paris. Member of Dada and Surrealism. Associate of Marcel Duchamp; co-founder of Société Anonyme with Duchamp and Katherine Dreier. Berenice Abbott worked as his assistant. Known for discovering/championing Eugene Atget.

clues.clue: Born Emmanuel Radnitzky
examples: name this American Dadaist who...was born Emmanuel Radnitzky
for 10 points, identify this surrealist and Dadaist who was born Emmanuel Radnitzky

clues.clue: Berenice Abbott worked as his assistant; both championed Eugene Atget's street photography
examples: Berenice Abbot worked as an assistant to this photographer, who, like her, championed the empty street views of Eugene Atget.
Abbott served as this artist's assistant while studying in Paris.

clues.clue: Passionate chess player; designed the first modern chess set of purely abstract geometrical forms
examples: This photographer and passionate chess player designed the first modern chess set to be composed entirely of abstract geometrical forms.

clues.clue: Self-portrait Autoportrait: a handprint on a white pentagon above a doorbell
examples: A self-portrait by this photographer consists of a handprint on a piece of cardboard above a doorbell.
This man put his handprint on a white pentagon in a work titled Autoportrait

clues.clue: Co-founded the Société Anonyme with Marcel Duchamp and Katherine Dreier
examples: This artist was a co-founder of the Societe Anonyme with Katherine Drier.

clues.clue: Bill Brandt studied in his studio

clues.clue: Made aerographs (1919 onwards) — paintings using an airbrush; Seguidilla was the first
examples: This artist's 1919 gouache called Seguidilla was the first of his 'aerographs,' which he made using an airbrush.
This Dadaist created a series of abstract paintings he called 'aerographs'

comprehensive_summary:
Man Ray (1890–1976), born Emmanuel Radnitzky in Philadelphia, was one of the most inventive and wide-ranging artists associated with both Dada and Surrealism. He spent the most significant decades of his career in Paris, where he became embedded in the avant-garde milieu alongside Marcel Duchamp, with whom he co-founded the Société Anonyme.

His most iconic photograph, Le Violon d'Ingres (1924), depicts his muse and lover Kiki de Montparnasse (Alice Ernestine Prin) — the 'Queen of Montparnasse' — nude from the back, with two f-holes painted on her skin to transform her body into a visual pun on a violin. In May 2022, it sold for the record price of $12.4 million. Another famous photograph, Noir et Blanche (1926), shows Kiki's white face resting horizontally next to an upright African mask, a juxtaposition of Western and African forms typical of Surrealist primitivism.

Man Ray was instrumental in developing cameraless photography. He named his version of photograms 'rayographs' after himself, creating images by placing objects such as springs, rolls of film, and a slinky directly on photosensitive paper. He also developed the solarization (Sabattier effect) technique together with his lover Lee Miller — partially reversing tonal values by exposing a developing image to a sudden flash of light. Lee Miller's torso appears in one of his photograms. Man Ray also photographed Meret Oppenheim nude with ink on her palm and forearm at an etching press, one of his celebrated series of photographs of that Surrealist artist.

As a Dadaist, Man Ray created several celebrated readymades and assemblages. The Gift (1921) is an iron with a row of metal tacks glued to the bottom, making it useless. The Enigma of Isidore Ducasse (1920) is a sewing machine wrapped in an army blanket and tied with yellow string, referencing Lautréamont's famous Surrealist image of an umbrella and a sewing machine meeting on a dissecting table. Object to Be Destroyed (1923) combined a metronome with a photograph of an eye; in 1957, student protesters actually destroyed the original in his Paris studio, after which Man Ray created an edition titled Indestructible Object.

Among Man Ray's collaborative and documentary work, his photographs of Marcel Duchamp dressed as the cross-dressing female alter-ego Rrose Selavy are iconic; one appeared on the label of the Belle Haleine perfume bottle. Man Ray helped discover and champion the Parisian street photographer Eugène Atget alongside Berenice Abbott, who worked as his studio assistant. Bill Brandt also trained in his studio. Man Ray was a passionate chess player and designed the first modern chess set composed entirely of abstract geometrical forms. His aerographs — paintings made with an airbrush — date from 1919, with Seguidilla as the first.
